label: Audio
title: Loading audio
lead: Load embedded and loose audio clips, reuse cached sounds, and wire them into buildings or AudioSource components.
bodyHtml:
Embedded audio

Use embedded audio when the clip is required for your mod to function: hit sounds, machine loops, voice lines, or other shipped defaults. Set the audio file's Build Action to Embedded Resource, then load it by suffix from the calling assembly.

AudioClip centrifugeHit = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedAudio("Audio.centrifuge-hit.wav");

AssetLoader caches embedded audio by assembly plus normalized resource path, so repeating the same load call returns the same clip instead of decoding it again.

Loose audio files

Use loose files when players should be able to swap clips without rebuilding your DLL, such as sound packs or optional ambience. LoadAudioFromPluginFolder() resolves a path relative to your plugin DLL.

AudioClip loop = AssetLoader.LoadAudioFromPluginFolder(this, "Audio/centrifuge-loop.wav");

The plugin-folder example points at BepInEx/plugins/MyMod/Audio/centrifuge-loop.wav. Loose file loads are cached by full file path so later calls reuse the same clip object.

Shared audio cache

Please cache your audio. Loading audio every time you want to use it is inefficient.

Luckily, there is a built-in way:

AudioClip embeddedLoop = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedAudio("Audio.centrifuge-loop.wav");
AssetLoader.CacheAudioClip("modname.centrifuge.loop", embeddedLoop);
AudioClip cachedLoop = AssetLoader.GetCachedAudioClip("modname.centrifuge.loop");

Using audio in gameplay

Loaded clips are standard Unity AudioClip objects. You can pass them into CUCoreLib definitions that accept clips or play them directly with CUCoreUtils.PlaySoundAt().

AudioClip centrifugeHit = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedAudio("Audio.centrifuge-hit.wav");

BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("centrifuge", new CustomBuildingEntityDefinition
{
    Name = "Centrifuge",
    Sprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.centrifuge.png", 8f),
    HitSound = centrifugeHit // Plays the audio clip on claw hit
});
CUCoreUtils.PlaySoundAt(
    AssetLoader.GetCachedAudioClip("yourmod.verycoolsound.effect"),
    volume: 0.7f,
    delay: 0.1f,
    position: (Vector2)transform.position,
    pitch: 1.1f
);
CUCoreUtils.Talk("Hello there.");
CUCoreUtils.TalkElectronic("Beep boop.", item);
Supported formats and tips
  • AssetLoader supports .wav, .mp1, .mp2, .mp3, .cue, .aif, and .aiff.
  • Sorry, .ogg enjoyers.
  • Like images, it is preferred to use embedded clips for required defaults and loose files for player-replaceable audio packs.
